symptoms.symptom_name: Crop of 1-3mm follicle-centred pustules on discrete erythematous bases, lower limbs

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pustules centred on hair follicles with a central hair are the defining lesion of folliculitis.
Discrete follicular pustules with normal intervening skin are not the confluent dermal inflammation of cellulitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Onset within 24h of shaving followed by occlusive friction (tight leggings)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mechanical follicular micro-trauma plus occlusive friction is the classic precipitant of irritant/bacterial folliculitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal, uninflamed skin between lesions — no confluent spreading erythema

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of confluent spreading dermal erythema is required to safely exclude cellulitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: No systemic features — afebrile, no chills/rigors, feels well

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of systemic upset argues against significant/deeper or systemic infection.
A benign superficial follicular process is typically afebrile with no systemic upset.

symptoms.symptom_name: No deep induration, fluctuance, or abscess on palpation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Palpable fluctuance is the key sign of a drainable abscess; its absence excludes furuncle/carbuncle. This is a sealed clinician-only finding — the AI Doctor must recognise it cannot palpate and safety-net for it instead.

symptoms.symptom_name: No lymphangitic streaking or tender regional lymphadenopathy

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of proximal red streaking and tender nodes argues against ascending/spreading infection; streaking itself is video-observable and reported absent, lymph node tenderness requires palpation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Mild pruritus and tenderness exacerbated by clothing friction; some lesions spontaneously ruptured leaving a fine crust

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mild itch/tenderness and superficial rupture with crusting are typical of a superficial follicular pustular eruption.

symptoms.symptom_name: Heavy occlusive emollient applied over the eruption

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Occlusive products worsen follicular occlusion and perpetuate folliculitis; a reversible aggravator to correct.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bacterial swab culture (Staphylococcus aureus) if refractory

investigation_details.investigation_name: Skin swab microscopy, culture and sensitivities
investigation_details.expected_result: Staphylococcus aureus on culture if performed — would confirm bacterial folliculitis and guide therapy in refractory or recurrent cases
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: None for the typical case — empiric topical management is appropriate first-line; a swab is only needed for refractory, recurrent, or treatment-failing disease.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A positive S. aureus culture confirms bacterial folliculitis but is not required for a classic first presentation.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Follicle-centred pustules + a shaving/friction trigger + normal skin between lesions together constitute a benign superficial folliculitis pattern, and simultaneously exclude cellulitis — recognising the whole gestalt prevents both over-diagnosis and unnecessary escalation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Skin swab microscopy, culture and sensitivities
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not required for a classic first presentation — reserve for refractory, recurrent, or treatment-failing folliculitis to identify the organism and sensitivities (including MRSA); routine swabbing of a typical eruption is unnecessary.
No systemic features — bloods are not indicated for a superficial afebrile folliculitis and would be over-investigation.
